libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated JVM tests with Test.jvmArgs including `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`. If tests exercise OGNL reflection against JDK collection internals, also add `--add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED`, matching OGNL's upstream JDK17+ test profile. Do not set `ognl.security.manager` or `ognl.UseJDK9PlusAccessHandler` globally unless a specific test needs that path; set and restore such properties inside the test.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: SecurityManager-based OGNL coverage is fragile on JDK 25 because SecurityManager APIs are terminally deprecated/being removed.
Javassist expression-compilation coverage may behave differently in native image than on the JVM; keep native-image expectations separate from JVM setup.
OGNL reads some system properties during static initialization, so property-based tests must isolate class initialization order.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL is an in-process expression/reflection library and its only runtime dependency, Javassist, is already transitive from ognl:ognl:3.3.5; no Docker or extra Maven dependency is needed. Meaningful JVM coverage of its reflection/expression-compiler paths does need JDK module-opening test setup on modern JDKs, which is also reflected by the existing OGNL test scaffold and upstream JDK17+ test profile.
